column1: # POLARIS RULES — repo policy as data. TAB-separated: scope<TAB>kind<TAB>pattern<TAB>message
#   scope   files_owned semantics: exact path · dir/ prefix · glob
#   kind    path    = scope is forbidden to write, even inside files_owned (danger zones)
#           content = added lines under scope must not match the ERE pattern (use - for path kind)
# Enforced at write time (Claude Code guard), at verify/handoff (any model), and at audit.
# EVOLVE proposes new lines from kickback/Learned evidence; a human approves; you append one line.
# THE BIG ONE: ops/ is POLARIS's own INSTALLATION — the board you are standing on. The PRODUCT is
# kit/. Every kit file below exists twice: once in kit/ (source, edit freely) and once in ops/
# (installed, never hand-edit). Editing the installed copy is always a mistake: it is silently
# overwritten the next time we install a release, so the work is lost — and until then the board is
# running code that exists nowhere in the source. The rules name the installed copy and point at the
# source. ops/board/, ops/contracts/ and the INIT artifacts are NOT listed: they are board state, and
# the Planner and Integrator must be able to write them.
ops/polaris
column4: installed copy — edit kit/ops/polaris; refresh with: python kit/ops/pack.py --dogfood

column1: ops/install.sh
ops/dashboard.py
ops/VERSION
column4: installed copy — it records which release we run; edit kit/ops/VERSION

ops/PROMPTS.md

# kit/ops/VERSION rule LIFTED 2026-07-15 — repo owner authorized assistant-driven releases. Still a release act: bump ONLY as a full release (VERSION + CHANGELOG + tag + dogfood together). Re-enable: restore this as a `path` rule.
# .github/ rule lifted ONCE 2026-07-16 (owner-approved, single commit) so the quiet-line drill could learn to count above the 5.9.1 install epilogue; restored immediately below.
.github/
column4: the CI drills are the last gate between a bad kit and every user — an agent must not edit its own tests
